name: Community National Bank
description_short: Community National Bank is an FDIC-insured Vermont community bank offering deposit accounts, electronic banking, home lending, and trust & investment services since 1851.
description_long: Community National Bank is a Vermont-based community bank that has operated since 1851, growing from its original $1,200 weekly business into a modern billion-dollar institution. The bank positions itself as Vermont's Community Bank, emphasizing local involvement and commitment to strengthening communities through banking services and charitable initiatives. While the website highlights mortgages prominently through dedicated residential lending pages and staff profiles, the bank's actual service portfolio extends well beyond mortgages to include traditional banking products. The bank offers FDIC-insured deposit accounts, electronic banking services, a mobile banking app, trust and investment services through CFSG, and special home equity programs. Two named residential lenders (Benjamin Brooks and Penelope L. Johnson) are featured with specific NMLS numbers and service territories across Vermont counties. The bank emphasizes community involvement, mentioning volunteer work, school partnerships, charitable foundation investments, and specific programs like Habitat for Humanity collaboration. Recent leadership changes are highlighted through CEO videos released in 2024-2025, with new President and CEO Christopher Caldwell promoting the bank's independence and community values. The website content focuses heavily on personal banking and mortgages rather than comprehensive banking service details, limiting visibility into the full breadth of their deposit account offerings, rates, or business banking services.
services: Trust and investment services through CFSG
Residential lending across specified Vermont counties
Mortgage rate quotes (by contact with residential lender)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Long institutional history since 1851 with substantial growth to billion-dollar asset size
Dedicated residential lending team with named NMLS-licensed loan officers and specific service territories
Mobile banking app available for convenient digital access
Special home equity programs explicitly offered for home improvement and renovation projects
Active community involvement through volunteering, school partnerships, and charitable foundation
Trust and investment services available through CFSG subsidiary
Multiple service locations across Vermont counties (Caledonia, Essex, Orange, Orleans, Washington, Franklin, Lamoille)
cons: Website does not display mortgage rates, requiring customers to contact for pricing
Limited information about deposit account types, minimum balances, fees, or interest rates
No clear information about business banking, commercial lending, or small business services
Residential lending appears geographically limited to specific Vermont counties only
Minimal details about personal loan products, credit card offerings, or other consumer lending beyond mortgages
best_for: Vermont homeowners seeking mortgage pre-qualification or refinancing with local relationship banking
Residents in Orleans, Essex, Franklin, Lamoille, Caledonia, Orange, and Washington Vermont counties
Customers seeking FDIC-insured deposit accounts with local community bank values
Homeowners interested in home equity lines or special equity programs for renovations
diagnosis: Community National Bank is best for Vermont residents seeking traditional FDIC-insured banking with a community-oriented lender, particularly those in Orleans, Essex, Franklin, Lamoille, Caledonia, Orange, and Washington counties. The main caveat is that the website emphasizes mortgages heavily while providing minimal disclosure about deposit account terms, rates, fees, and the full range of banking services, requiring direct contact for most product information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
